vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely inaccurate regarding the current state of the biome. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent or entirely dormant/submerged. P2 (Mexican Mint) is not visible as a distinct specimen. P3 (Pothos) is present, anchored by the white rabbit. P4 (Silver Guest) is not identifiable as a distinct plant.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No visible foliage in the yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No visible foliage in the black pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present: Pothos specimen remains, though showing significant decline.

plant_audit.P1_Yellow_Pot: Empty/Soil only. No signs of life.
plant_audit.P2_Black_Pot: Empty/Soil only. No signs of life.
plant_audit.P3_Pothos: The specimen has undergone severe chlorosis and structural collapse. The leaf visible in the earliest image has withered and effectively vanished by the current state.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of eggshell fragments in the central black pot (P3/P4 area).
biome_observations.surface_anomalies: A small electronic component (pin header) was present in the early sequence and has since been removed.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-1: Progressive loss of the Pothos leaf structure. The stem has retracted or decayed.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_Current: Total loss of the primary Pothos leaf. The plant is now reduced to a vestigial stem or has completely perished.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of terminal decline. The lack of photosynthetic tissue suggests the plants have either succumbed to root rot (overwatering) or extreme light deficiency. The 'String of Nickels' and 'Mexican Mint' are effectively non-existent in the current visual data.
anomalies: Eggshell fragments used as soil amendment/top dressing.
Electronic component (pin header) found on the desk surface in early images.
Total absence of expected plant life in designated pots.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical environment has suffered a catastrophic failure. Starting from the earliest image, the Pothos (P3) was already in a weakened state, and over the 5-day period, it has completely collapsed. The other registered plants (P1, P2, P4) are not visible, suggesting they were either never established or have fully decomposed into the soil. The environment is currently a collection of empty, damp pots.
